groups.name: Name
groups.listHint: Groups can be used when setting ACL for plugins.
groups.noOrg: Select an organization to manage groups.
groups.membersHelpInline: Enter userId for members in the organization, separated by commas or new lines.
groups.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, email or userId
groups.searchHint: Start typing to filter members and add via checkbox.

groupPermissions.title: Group Permissions
groupPermissions.description: Manage module permissions for each organization group. Groups with permissions can access specific modules.
groupPermissions.noOrg: Select an organization to manage group permissions.
groupPermissions.empty: No groups found. Create groups in the Groups section first.

info.title: How group permissions work
info.description: Group permissions let you grant specific access rights to modules based on group membership. Perfect for organizing access by roles, departments, or projects.
info.useCase1: Give the support group access to DNS management but not certificates
info.useCase2: Let the development group manage all modules while customer service only sees specific ones
info.useCase3: Create project groups with time-limited access to specific modules
info.note: Permissions are inherited from higher levels if nothing else is specified. Explicit settings at group level override inherited permissions.

help.title: How group permissions work
help.grantDesc: Members of this group explicitly receive this permission for the module.
help.denyDesc: Members of this group are explicitly blocked from this permission.
help.inheritDesc: No explicit setting. Access is determined by module policies and other group memberships.

organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.description: Switch active organization and view roles.
organizations.noActiveOrg: No organization is active right now. Select one from the list to view organization-specific settings.
organizations.noOrganizations: No organizations are linked to the current provider. Add an organization or switch provider in the context switcher.

delegations.title: Delegations
delegations.description: Manage MSP-to-organization delegations without adding members to the organization.

info.title: What are delegations?
info.description: Delegations give external users access to specific modules without making them members of the organization. Perfect for consultants, web agencies, or partners who need limited access.
info.useCase1: Give an external consultant time-limited access to DNS management
info.useCase2: Let a web agency manage certificates or redirects without full access
info.useCase3: Share specific module permissions with partners or vendors
info.note: Unlike members, delegated users do not get access to the organization's settings or member list.

form.title: New delegation
form.description: Assign module permissions to a user without organization membership.

form.existingDelegationNote: Permissions from all active delegations are combined (union). This can be intentional if you want to give different permissions for different purposes or time periods.
form.modulesDescription: Select which modules the user should have access to
form.modulesSelected: {modules} module selected ({permissions} permissions) | {modules} modules selected ({permissions} permissions)

form.selectModulesFirst: Select at least one module in step 1 to continue.
form.selectPermission: Select at least one module or permission.

auth.title: Auth & SSO
auth.description: Enable Identity Provider, configure OpenID or Entra and control whether SSO should be required.
auth.pageDescription: Select Identity Provider, follow the guide and control whether SSO should be required for the organization.
auth.noActiveOrg: You need to select an active organization before you can configure SSO.

idp.title: Identity Provider
idp.description: Select which IdP should be active and fill in OIDC details. The organization can only have one IdP at a time.
idp.redirectUriHint: Fill in the information according to the guide. Redirect URL for this organization:

wantAssertionsSigned.title: Require signed assertions
wantAssertionsSigned.description: Ensures that IdP signs the responses.

requireSsoDisabled.noIdp: Configure an IdP before SSO can be required.
requireSsoDisabled.incomplete: Fill in all required IdP fields to be able to require SSO.

validation.incomplete: Fill in all required IdP fields before saving.

steps.0: Create a new OIDC app in your IdP (e.g. OneLogin) and select Web client.
steps.1: Set {redirect} as Redirect URL in your IdP.
steps.2: Copy Issuer/Discovery URL, Client ID and Client Secret from the IdP.
steps.3: Fill in these values in CloudPortal under Auth settings for your organization.
steps.4: Test the login before enabling "Require SSO" in CloudPortal.

steps.0: Register a new Entra app (App registration) and select Web as platform.
steps.1: Set Redirect URI to {redirect} in Microsoft Entra ID.
steps.2: Note Application (client) ID, Directory (tenant) ID and create a Client secret.
steps.3: Fill in these values in CloudPortal under Auth settings for your organization.
steps.4: Assign users and test the login, then fill in Tenant ID, Client ID/Secret here before requiring SSO.

steps.0: Create a new SAML app in your IdP and set ACS/Callback URL to the redirect URL above.
steps.1: Copy Issuer/Entity ID, Entry Point (SSO URL) and public X.509 certificate from the IdP.
steps.2: Fill in these values in CloudPortal under Auth settings for your organization.
steps.3: Enter any Audience/Entity ID expected by the IdP and select whether AuthnRequests should be signed.
steps.4: Test the login before enabling "Require SSO" in CloudPortal.

requireSso.title: Require SSO
requireSso.description: All users must log in via selected IdP.

allowLocalLogin.title: Allow local login for owners
allowLocalLogin.description: Keep fallback via password for owners.

features.idp: Select IdP type (OpenID or Entra AD)
features.cloudflare: Follow the guide to configure Cloudflare Access
features.require: Enable "Require SSO" when IdP is ready
